ul.li: Host: Input/output buffers + 2 pinned staging buffers
Device: 2 input buffers + intermediate + output + window + work area
No ring buffers: Direct pipeline flow
Host: Input/output buffers + pinned ring buffers (98 KB, direct DMA via peek_frame())
Ring buffers: Per-channel pinned memory — zero-copy H2D (Phase 1.1)
Use case: Real-time streaming with arbitrary chunk sizes

div.p: Both modes use the same device memory layout and processing pipeline. The streaming overhead is entirely in host memory for ring buffer management.

StreamingExecutor Mode: Low-latency streaming, arbitrary chunk sizes Total Memory: ~262 KB (nfft=2048, channels=2) +60% vs BatchExecutor Phase 1.1: Ring buffers in pinned memory — zero-copy DMA via peek_frame()

ul.li: Ring buffers: 3 × nfft × channels × sizeof(float) = 98 KB (extra)
Device buffers: same as BatchExecutor = 164 KB

div.p: Tradeoffs: ✅ Supports arbitrary input chunk sizes ✅ Continuous streaming capability ✅ Per-channel independence ✅ Zero-copy H2D DMA via peek_frame() (Phase 1.1) ❌ +60% memory overhead

ol.li: peek_frame(nfft) - Zero-copy FrameView into pinned memory
advance(hop_size) - Move read pointer (after D2H sync)

ul.li: Allows safe accumulation before first extraction
